name: Pro V1
description: Titleist flagship 3-piece urethane tour ball. The best-selling premium tour ball worldwide.

name: Pro V1x
description: Higher flight and firmer feel variant of the Pro V1. 4-piece construction, 348 dimples.

name: AVX
description: Lower flight, lower spin, softer feel than Pro V1. 3-piece GRN41 cast thermoset urethane.

name: Tour Soft
description: Titleist's largest core golf ball for distance and soft feel. 4CE urethane cover.

name: TruFeel
description: Titleist's softest feel golf ball. 2-piece with TruFlex cover technology.

name: Velocity
description: Fast, high-flying distance ball. 2-piece NaZ2 ionomer cover.

name: Tour Speed
description: Speed-focused tour ball at a lower price than Pro V1. 3-piece urethane.

name: Chrome Soft
description: Callaway's flagship tour ball. 4-piece with Graphene-infused Dual SoftFast Core and SoftFeel urethane cover.

name: Chrome Soft X
description: Firmer and higher spinning variant of Chrome Soft, designed for faster swing speeds.

name: Chrome Tour
description: Successor to Chrome Soft with Precision Technology for consistent flight.

name: Supersoft
description: Callaway's ultra-low compression distance ball. 2-piece HEX aerodynamics.

name: Warbird
description: High-energy core distance ball for maximum length off the driver.

name: ERC Soft
description: 3-piece with GRIP-IT urethane cover designed for mid-speed players.

name: TP5
description: TaylorMade's flagship 5-layer cast urethane tour ball. Used by Rory McIlroy.

name: TP5x
description: Higher launching, less spinning variant of TP5. 5-layer construction.

name: Tour Response
description: 3-piece cast urethane ball offering tour performance at a lower price point.

name: Distance Plus
description: 2-piece ionomer distance ball for maximum yardage.

name: Soft Response
description: 2-piece low compression ball with ultra-soft feel.

name: Z-Star
description: Srixon's flagship 3-piece tour ball with 338 Speed Dimple pattern and 72 Alignment.

name: Z-Star XV
description: Higher spin, firmer feel variant of Z-Star. Extra high velocity for faster swingers.

name: Q-Star Tour
description: 3-piece urethane ball balancing distance and greenside control.

name: Q-Star
description: 2-piece ionomer ball offering good distance and feel at a mid-tier price.

name: Soft Feel
description: Srixon's low-compression recreational ball for maximum softness.

name: Tour B XS
description: Bridgestone's premium soft tour ball. Used by Tiger Woods. Reactive urethane cover.

name: Tour B X
description: Firmer, higher-spinning tour ball for fast swing speeds. 330 Dual Dimple design.

name: Tour B RX
description: Designed for moderate swing speeds (under 105 mph). 4-piece gradational core.

name: Tour B RXS
description: Softer Tour B RX for players prioritising feel over distance.

name: e12 Speed
description: 3-piece Active Acceleration Mantle for distance. 326 Seamless Golf Dimple.

name: e12 Soft
description: Softer e12 variant with Contact Force Dimple for improved ball contact.

name: Pro Plus
description: Vice's premium tour ball with 4-piece construction and cast urethane cover.

name: Pro
description: Vice's flagship 3-piece tour ball balancing distance, control, and value.

name: Drive
description: Vice's high-energy 2-piece distance ball.

name: Staff Model
description: Wilson's premium urethane tour ball with 362 dimple pattern.

name: Duo Soft
description: Ultra-low compression (29) soft feel ball for the slowest swing speeds.

name: Duo Optix
description: Coloured variant of Duo Soft for maximum visibility.

name: Vivid
description: Volvik's signature matte-finish coloured ball. 3-piece urethane in multiple vivid colours.

name: Chrome Tour
description: Volvik's premium glossy tour ball with nano-coating technology.

name: RB Tour
description: Mizuno's flagship 4-piece urethane tour ball with C-Dimple technology.

name: RB Tour X
description: Firmer, higher spinning RB Tour variant for faster swing players.

name: RB 566
description: Distance ball with 566 micro C-Dimples for maximum aerodynamic efficiency.

name: 20XI
description: Nike's last premium tour ball before exiting the equipment market. RZN core technology.

name: One Platinum
description: Nike's premium 3-piece tour ball from the One family.

name: MTB-X
description: Snell's premium 3-piece urethane tour ball with ZiO core for high spin players.

name: MTB Black
description: Snell's 3-piece urethane tour ball focused on low spin and maximum distance.

name: Rush
description: Pinnacle's high-energy distance ball with 332 tetrahedral dimple design.

name: Soft
description: Pinnacle's low compression recreational ball emphasising soft feel.

name: Gold
description: Pinnacle's classic distance ball. One of the best-selling value balls in the US.

name: Gamer
description: Top Flite's premium offering with 3-piece construction for better players.

name: XL
description: Top Flite's classic high-energy distance ball.

name: Tour
description: Maxfli's premium 4-piece urethane tour ball.

name: SoftFli
description: Ultra-low compression (35) recreational ball for soft feel.

name: Vero X1
description: OnCore's premium 4-piece urethane tour ball with polycarbonate inner core.

name: ELIXR
description: OnCore's 3-piece urethane ball for moderate swing speeds.

name: Avant
description: OnCore's 3-piece urethane ball designed for mid-speed players seeking tour-level short game performance.

name: AD333
description: Srixon's mid-tier 2-piece ball popular in Europe, offering distance and durability.

name: AD333 Tour
description: Srixon's 3-piece urethane version of the AD333, adding greenside spin at a performance price.

name: e6
description: Bridgestone's straight-distance ball designed for golfers with moderate swing speed and off-centre hits.

name: Vice Tour
description: Vice Golf's premium 4-piece cast urethane tour ball offering maximum greenside control.

name: Vice Soft
description: Vice's 2-piece low-compression ball for players seeking a softer feel at a direct-to-consumer price.

name: Vice Speed
description: Vice's 2-piece distance ball optimised for maximum ball speed off the driver.

name: Staff Model R
description: Wilson's 4-piece tour ball tuned for mid-spin with enhanced greenside control. The softer, lower-spinning sibling of Staff Model.

name: Noodle Easy Distance
description: TaylorMade's Noodle entry-level distance ball offering consistent flight for beginner to mid-handicap players.

name: Noodle Long & Soft
description: Noodle's ultra-soft distance ball combining maximum length with a noticeably soft feel.

name: Noodle Ice
description: Cold-weather optimised Noodle designed to maintain performance in low-temperature conditions.

name: D2
description: Top Flite's dedicated distance ball with 2-piece construction for maximum straight distance.

name: Tour X
description: Maxfli's hardest-spinning 4-piece urethane ball for faster swing speeds demanding high spin.

name: Get Sum
description: Snell's value-tier 2-piece ball offering solid performance at a budget-friendly direct-to-consumer price.

name: Tour 900
description: Inesis (Decathlon) premium tour ball with urethane cover, engineered for experienced golfers.

name: Distance 500
description: Inesis mid-range 2-piece distance ball for regular golfers seeking extra length.

name: Soft 500
description: Inesis low-compression soft-feel ball for players prioritising a comfortable sensation at impact.

name: Speedsoft
description: TaylorMade's entry-level urethane ball offering a soft feel at an accessible price, bridging the gap between recreational and performance categories.

name: Signature
description: Costco Kirkland Signature golf ball offering tour-level performance at a dramatically lower price point. Famous for disrupting the premium ball market.

name: DDH Tour One
description: Dunlop's premium 3-piece urethane tour ball for the Japanese and Asian markets.

name: Raw Distance
description: Slazenger's 2-piece distance ball for everyday recreational golfers.

name: Laddie Extreme
description: Precept's budget-friendly 2-piece distance ball, popular as a pond ball. Precept was absorbed by Bridgestone.

name: NXT Tour
description: Titleist's mid-tier 2-piece ionomer ball positioned below Pro V1, offering good distance and feel for mid-handicap players.

name: NXT Tour S
description: Softer version of the NXT Tour with enhanced feel around the greens. 2-piece with surlyn cover.

name: DT TruSoft
description: Titleist's entry-level soft ball with very low compression for golfers seeking maximum softness at an accessible price.

name: Chrome Soft Triple Track
description: Chrome Soft variant featuring the Triple Track alignment system — three parallel Callaway blue lines for putting alignment.

name: Chrome Soft X LS
description: Low-spin variant of Chrome Soft X designed for faster swing speeds seeking a lower, more penetrating ball flight.

name: Supersoft Magma
description: Vividly coloured Supersoft variant in orange and other Magma colours, same ultra-low compression formula as standard Supersoft.

name: Hex Chrome
description: Callaway's mid-tier 3-piece ionomer ball with HEX aerodynamics, bridging the gap between Supersoft and Chrome Soft.

name: TP5 PIX
description: TP5 variant with TaylorMade's Pix alignment system — geometric pattern of dots on the ball surface for enhanced alignment.

name: Tour Response Stripe
description: Tour Response with a high-visibility stripe alignment aid printed directly on the ball for improved putting setup.

name: Project (a)
description: TaylorMade's mid-performance 3-piece ionomer ball aimed at the aspiring golfer seeking tour-like feel without full tour ball pricing.

name: Z-Star Diamond
description: Srixon's highest-tier tour ball above Z-Star XV, featuring 4-piece construction and FastLayer core for maximum spin and control.

name: Q-Star Tour Divide
description: Q-Star Tour variant with a distinctive two-tone Divide hemisphere paint pattern for enhanced visibility and alignment.

name: e7
description: Bridgestone's straightness-focused distance ball for moderate swing speeds, predecessor to the e12 line.

name: B XS
description: Bridgestone's short-lived 4-piece tour ball predecessor to the Tour B XS line, with Reactiv urethane cover.

name: Extra Soft
description: Bridgestone's low-compression recreational ball for slower swing speeds seeking a soft feel at an accessible price.

name: Pro DRIP
description: Vice Pro variant featuring a distinctive drip/splatter paint design, same core performance as the standard Pro.

name: Pro Zero
description: Minimalist, no-logo version of the Vice Pro with plain white finish and no branding for a clean aesthetic.

name: Staff Model X
description: Wilson's firmer, higher-spinning tour ball for faster swing speed players demanding maximum greenside spin.

name: ZIP
description: Wilson's long-running 2-piece distance ball known for durability and consistent flight.

name: Vivid XT
description: Volvik's firmer, higher-compression variant of the Vivid in the same matte-finish coloured format.

name: S4
description: Volvik's premium tour-level urethane ball in a glossy finish, targeting better players seeking greenside control.

name: Power Soft
description: Volvik's low-compression recreational ball combining soft feel with the brand's characteristic vibrant colours.

name: RB Max
description: Mizuno's distance-focused ball for moderate swing speeds with a large core and low-drag aerodynamics.

name: RB Max Soft
description: Softer companion to the RB Max with lower compression for players prioritising feel and softer impact sensation.

name: RB 566 V
description: Voluminous micro-dimple variant of the RB 566 with an even higher dimple count for consistent aerodynamics.

name: RZN
description: Nike's RZN-technology tour balls featuring a reactive polymer lattice core for energy efficiency. Discontinued when Nike exited equipment.

name: One Tour
description: Nike's premium 4-piece tour ball from the One family, predecessor to the RZN line.

name: Mojo
description: Nike's colourful recreational 2-piece ball available in multiple bright colours for visibility and fun.

name: Tour S
description: Maxfli's softer tour ball, a lower-compression alternative to the Tour X for players preferring a softer feel.

name: MTB Red
description: Snell's softer, lower-compression MTB variant designed for moderate swing speeds and players who prefer a softer feel.

name: Vero X2
description: OnCore's next-generation premium tour ball successor to the Vero X1, with refined aerodynamics and updated cover compound.

name: Gamer Tour
description: Top Flite's urethane-covered performance ball positioned above the standard Gamer, aimed at better players.
